caption: Figure 1: HyperFitS: Top: The HyperFitS strategy showing a hypernetwork tak- ing baseline and water suppression correction parameters and predicting weights for a quantification network with a physics-based encoder. Bottom: The phys- ical model combining metabolite and macromolecule basis set, baseline spline functions, and water suppression correction factor used to predict the spectral fit.
description2: The figure depicts the HyperFitS strategy, featuring a hypernetwork predicting weights for a quantification network with physics-based encoder and physical model combining metabolite and macromolecule basis sets.
description3: The multi-panel plot displays a hypernetwork strategy and a physical model for spectral fit prediction.
questions: What role does the hypernetwork play in the overall quantification strategy?
How does the physical model incorporate baseline and water suppression corrections?

caption: Figure 2: A qualitative comparison showing metabolic maps of NAA+NAAG, Cr+PCr, GPC+PCh, inositol, and Glu+Gln, at 7T quantified with HyperFitS and LCModel. Individual representative spectra are displayed below. Process- ing times for a single MRSI volume of HyperFitS and LCModel are 10sec vs ∼1h for 3.4mm, and 90sec vs ∼8h for 2mm
description2: Metabolic maps comparing NAA+NAAG, Cr+PCr, GPC+PCh, inositol, and Glu+Gln are plotted at 7T using HyperFitS and LCModel.
description3: The figure presents a multi-panel plot displaying metabolic maps and spectra for several compounds across different resolutions and quantification methods.
questions: What metabolic compounds are shown in the comparison of brain maps?
How do the displayed maps differ based on quantification method?

caption: Figure 3: Metabolic maps of NAA+NAAG, Cr+PCr, GPC+PCh, Inositol, and Glu+Gln quantified with HyperFitS and LCModel from 3D MRSI acquired at 3T in a glioma patient. Maps are compared for different baseline modeling. Individual representative spectra are displayed below.
description2: Metabolic maps of NAA+NAAG, Cr+PCr, GPC+PCh, Inositol, and Glu+Gln quantified with HyperFitS and LCModel from 3D MRSI acquired at 3T in a glioma patient are displayed.
description3: A multi-panel plot displays metabolic maps and representative spectra for NAA, Cr, GPC, Inositol, and Glu, using HyperFitS and LCModel.
questions: How do different baseline modeling approaches affect the representation of metabolic compounds?
What are the key metabolic compounds displayed in these maps?

caption: Figure 4: Top: A representative spectrum fitted by varying the hyperparam- eters for baseline flexibility (vertical) and spline node distance (horizontal) in increments of 1 10 of the total range. Three cases are shown enlarged on the right. The original spectrum is white, fit is red, baseline is yellow, and spline nodes are blue. Bottom: Contour plots of metabolite concentrations. A subject acquired at 7T with 3.4mm resolution was evaluated for 100 different baseline configurations. Baseline flexibility and spline node distance were changed over the hyperparameter range with increments of 1 10 of the total range. Values show the difference to the center value (0.5 flexibility, 0.15 ppm node distance) in per- cent.
description2: A representative spectrum is plotted with varying hyperparameters for baseline flexibility and spline node distance, featuring three enlarged cases on the right.
description3: A multi-panel plot displays fitted spectra with varying baseline flexibility and spline node distance, alongside contour plots of metabolite concentrations.
questions: How does varying baseline flexibility and node distance affect the fitted spectrum?
What is the relationship between baseline flexibility, node distance, and metabolite concentrations?

caption: Figure 5: Metabolite concentration boxplots for metabolites NAA+NAAG, Cr+PCr, PCh+GPC, Glu+Gln, Ins, Tau, GSH, and GABA. Evaluations are performed for 7T (top) and 3T (bottom). The 7T was evaluated with water sup- pression correction by HyperFitS. The 3T was processed with different baseline configurations by HyperFitS. Comparison to LCModel is included.
description2: The figure displays boxplots of metabolite concentration for eight metabolites at two magnetic field strengths, 7T and 3T, with varying processing methods.
description3: The figure presents two multi-panel boxplots comparing metabolite concentrations across different processing methods for 7T and 3T data.
questions: What is the primary comparison made regarding metabolite concentrations at different field strengths?
How do different processing configurations affect the observed metabolite concentration distributions?

caption: Figure 6: Uncertainty maps for the metabolites NAA, Glu and Ins at 7T (left) and 3T (right). LCModel uncertainties are computed as CRLBs, HyperFitS uncertainties are shown with Aleatoric and Epistemic uncertainty.
description2: Uncertainty maps plot metabolites NAA, Glu, and Ins at 7T and 3T magnetic field strengths, displaying LCModel and HyperFitS uncertainties.
description3: A multi-panel plot displays uncertainty maps for NAA, Glu, and Ins, comparing LCModel CRLBs with Aleatoric and Epistemic uncertainties at 7T and 3T.
questions: How do uncertainty maps for metabolites differ between 7T and 3T imaging?
What types of uncertainty are visualized for the metabolites NAA, Glu, and Ins?

caption: Figure 7: Correlation matrix of in-vivo metabolite concentrations fitted by Hy- perFitS at a.) 7T and b.) 3T. c.) A spectrum acquired at 7T with its fit and individual metabolite components.
description2: The figure is a correlation matrix of in-vivo metabolite concentrations fitted by HyperFitS at different magnetic field strengths, including 7T and 3T.
description3: The image contains two correlation matrices (a and b) and a spectrum with fit and component lines (c), all related to metabolite concentrations.
questions: How do metabolite concentrations relate to each other in vivo at different magnetic field strengths?
What is the overall pattern of correlations observed between different metabolite components?
